name: Jennifer Huynh
comments: "A friend and tried the trial period and like it. Honestly the cost is what drove us away 8 x a month for $220 is a bit steep for a budget and even with the credit given it still is quite expensive. I have gym pass that mean I would being almost $300 to keep both it doesn’t makes sense for me. Even if I didn’t have gym pass it would be to costly to justify it." Not interested in dropping to 4x or class packs

name: Katya Pineda
comments: Only wanted 1 month with the $50 credit. Talked to her about staying longer but she says she's "leaving Houston" and will not be able to continue classes with us. When discussing the cancellation notice with her, she claimed that someone at the front desk told her when she signed up that they would only put her membership on for 1 month. I asked if she could let me know who she spoke to and she said she didn't remember. Cancelled the membership for her as I didn't want to keep going back and forth and have her leave a bad review when she was adamant that's what she was told (which I could see happening with the confusion over the cancellations at that time- wouldn't happen now).

name: Bibi Gonzalez

name: Kristen Harootunian
comments: Going through fertility treatments and can't workout

comments: Loved her experience with everyone at the studio but misses her "traditional Pilates" classes too much and wants to go to a studio where she can take just Pilates daily.

name: Sarah Peters
comments: Injured her shoulder months ago and hasn't been able to workout. Started with a suspension to see if shoulder would heal and it hasn't. She called in and told her to send the email to make sure we had it in writing. She did submit the email on 1/5. Paid on 12/31 for final month that she will not be attending and cancellation will be effective 1/31.

name: Brooke Jackson
comments: Lost her job and can't afford the membership. Was failure to pay and said she cancelled the card and wasn't sure how we were able to run it, so I think she was trying to avoid paying the last month but the charge did go through today so I told her we would take it as her 30 day notice.

name: Claudia Kim
comments: Failure to pay and no response after multiple attempts

name: Vanesa Martinez
comments: Going through a divorce and has to move closer to the city and will be too far from studio

name: Emily Truong
comments: Submitted request to have her account deleted on 1/3. DND on all channels so not able to get in contact with her. Taking deletion request on 1/3 as 30 day cancellation notice.

name: Adele Castillo
comments: Enjoyed the studio and "the great people here" but "has other priorities she needs to focus on right now"  - ASKED TO DELETE CONTACT INFORMATION OF APP

name: Mario Fernandez
comments: Initial request submitted 12/29. Talked to him again 1/8. Loves the studio and loves the results he has seen but got a new promotion at work and he's now going to traveling for work overseas a lot more. Wants to purchase ClassPacks with us to use when he's in town and has been looking into other STRONG locations for the cities he will be frequenting. Was very sad to cancel

name: Mia Uriarte
comments: Loved the studio but is joining another family membership to cut costs

name: Hadya Ahmed
comments: Submitted her cancellation notice on 1/6 due to personal reasons. She then hurt her ankle and had to pause for a month while recovering per doctor's request. Paused account and will apply the month that she couldn't use because of injury (during pause) towards last month of membership

name: Lana Alaamari
comments: One of the more bizarre cancellations I have come across. Her husband? Fiance? called and was pretending to be her. I asked a few times if it was Lana and he said yes. He was adamant that her membership needed to be cancelled during her suspension. I told him that was not possible and she would have to do 1 more billing before cancellation. She then came into the studio so I talked to her in person. She did say she wanted to come to classes as she has enjoyed them but she "had to cancel" and wouldn't say much else. I think maybe he made her cancel? Offered class packs to keep coming.

name: Dee Pasquet
comments: First reached out on Instagram to cancel - after months of suspension after her dad died. Gave 1 month notice as it doesn't "fit into her schedule anymore". Tried to talk to her about downgrading instead of cancelling all together but she said she wants to cancel for now and re-join later

name: Symphony Cheeks
comments: Becoming a Pilates instructor and is required to take classes through the certifying studio until the fall when she graduates. Loved the studio (said we gave her the courage to sign up)

name: Sandra Olison
comments: Was on suspension due to finances and the possibility of being laid off (her company cut 65% of their employees). Re-activated for 1 more month but had to purchase a new car unexpectedly and has to cancel for now. Wants to return in a few months when she recovers a bit from buying the car. Over the next month while she's in studio we will discuss class packs and even 4x a month membership

name: Brigitte Hu
comments: Payment was declined. When contacting her to collect payment, she said her husband was ill and she didn't understand why we were trying to collect payment when she hasn't been coming into the studio. Told her about the 30 day cancellation and she stopped responding

name: Jalyn Amakwe
comments: Jalyn is one of the members that we have been in contact with because she hasn't come to the studio since August but has been paying since then. She called on the phone but I did finally get her written notice to process cancellation.

name: Erica Sauceda
comments: Was in a car accident and hurt her back. Has still been coming to classes but it's hurting too badly and she wants to go to PT before resuming. Offered to pause but she preferred to put in cancellation

name: Tiffany King
comments: Failed to pay 2/20. Cancelled her for failed payment. Traveling to Nigeria for family emergency

name: Cynthia Rivas
comments: Work schedule changed and she hasn't been able to come to the studio as much as she was before. Talked to her about downgrading but she doesn't want to keep paying until she knows exactly when she can commit to coming. Hoping her work schedule will change in the near future to be able to come back.

name: Annabel Gbededo
comments: Husband is now getting benefits through work to provide family membership at Lifetime so they decided as a family to cut the cost on STRONG membership. Loved the studio and the classes. DId ask her about downgrading but she said not at this time, told her to consider class packs so she can still particpate occasionally

name: Isabella Rocha
comments: Her friend, Kiara L. was paying for her membership but can no longer pay for both and Isabella can't pay it on her own. Talked to her about downgrading but she wasn't able to make it work

name: Gigi Covarrubias
comments: Gigi was late for a class and Ebbanee wasn't able to let her in late (there were reverse springs being used so she didn't want to cause a safety issue). Gigi then put in a cancellation notice. I talked to her on the phone and she did confirm that she has had a good in-studio experience and understood why she couldn't be let in late with the reverse springs but she's already been trying out other studios and wants to have more flexibility to go to multiple. Would like to continue with us on class packs to offer flexibility

name: Alicia Arvizu
